title: Master GoHighLevel Credentials: Share Certificates Like a Pro
description: Master GoHighLevel credentials & share certificates professionally. Learn setup, customization, LinkedIn best practices, and how modern design builds agency credibility.
html_content:
Your agency just launched an amazing certification program or course, and your members are buzzing about their achievements. But here's the problem: those old, clunky certificate designs don't scream "professional." They don't inspire your students to share their credentials on LinkedIn. They don't build your agency's brand authority the way they should.

That's where GoHighLevel's Credential Preview Experience changes the game. This modernized feature transforms how you issue, customize, and share certificates—making them so sleek and professional that your members actually *want* to broadcast their achievements to the world.

In this guide, I'll walk you through exactly how to master credential sharing in GoHighLevel, why the new design matters for your agency's credibility, and the best practices to get your certificates in front of the right audience. Ready to level up? Start your free 30-day trial here—that's double the standard trial length.

What is GoHighLevel's Credential Preview Experience?

GoHighLevel's Credential Preview Experience is a modernized system for designing, issuing, and sharing digital certificates and credentials. Unlike outdated PDF certificates that sit in inboxes and get forgotten, this new feature creates visually stunning, shareable credentials that your members are proud to display.

The key difference? These credentials are designed for the modern digital world. They're mobile-responsive, social-media friendly, and built to be verified—making them far more valuable to both your students and your agency's reputation.

Think of it this way: a boring PDF certificate says "you completed something." A modern GoHighLevel credential says "I'm a verified expert in this field," and your members will naturally share it across their professional networks.

💡 Pro Tip

The most successful agencies use credentials as a marketing multiplier. Every certificate shared is essentially free advertising for your programs. When your certified members showcase their credentials on LinkedIn, they're building your agency's visibility—at no additional cost to you.

How to Set Up and Customize Credentials in GoHighLevel

Setting up credentials in GoHighLevel is straightforward, but there are some customization best practices that separate pro agencies from the rest.

Step 1: Access Your Credentials Module

Log into your GoHighLevel account and navigate to your membership or course section. Look for the "Credentials" or "Certificates" tab. This is where you'll manage all aspects of your digital credentials.

Step 2: Choose Your Template

GoHighLevel offers pre-designed templates that are already optimized for the modern Credential Preview Experience. Select a template that aligns with your brand. These templates are responsive and look professional on desktop, mobile, and when shared on social platforms.

Step 3: Customize with Your Branding

This is crucial. Upload your agency logo, choose your brand colors, and customize the credential name. Your credentials should be instantly recognizable as coming from *your* agency. Include:

  • Your agency/company logo (high resolution)
  • Brand colors that match your website and marketing materials
  • A clear credential title (e.g., "GoHighLevel Certified Expert" or "Advanced Marketing Automation Specialist")
  • A brief description of what the credential validates

Step 4: Set Expiry Parameters

Decide if your credentials expire. Many agencies use 1-2 year expiry dates to encourage members to pursue continuing education. GoHighLevel makes this configurable, so you control the timeline.

Step 5: Enable Verification

One of the most powerful features: verification links. When you enable credential verification, anyone can visit a unique URL to confirm that the credential is legitimate and not expired. This builds massive trust.

Best Practices for Sharing Certificates on LinkedIn and Social Media

The real magic happens when your members share their credentials. Here's how to make sharing irresistible.

LinkedIn is Prime Real Estate

LinkedIn's credential feature integrates beautifully with GoHighLevel certificates. When your members receive their credentials, they can instantly add them to their LinkedIn profile. This positions them as verified experts and builds social proof for both them and your agency.

Encourage members to:

  • Add the credential to their LinkedIn profile immediately
  • Write a post about earning the credential (tag your agency)
  • Include the verification link in their post for transparency

Make Sharing a One-Click Process

In GoHighLevel's Credential Preview, there are built-in social share buttons. Your members should see easy options to share on LinkedIn, Twitter, Facebook, and via email. Test these buttons regularly to ensure they work seamlessly.

Timing Matters

Send credentials immediately upon course completion. The faster your members receive them, the fresher the excitement and the higher the likelihood they'll share. A credential that arrives weeks later loses momentum.

Create a Sharing Template

Provide your members with a suggested LinkedIn post template or caption they can use. Something like:

"I just earned my [Credential Name] certification from [Your Agency Name]. Excited to apply these skills and help more [target audience]. Learn more about becoming certified: [verification link]"

Downloading Certificates as PNG Files and Managing Expiry

Beyond sharing links, your members may want to download their certificates as high-resolution image files for presentations, websites, or portfolios.

PNG Download Functionality

GoHighLevel allows members to download their credentials as PNG files directly from the Credential Preview page. These are print-ready and web-ready, maintaining quality whether displayed digitally or framed on an office wall.

Pro agencies also provide a branded folder or shared drive where members can access and download their credentials at any time—this increases perceived value and reduces support requests.

Understanding Credential Expiry Behavior

If you've set expiry dates on credentials, here's what happens:

  • Members receive a notification before expiry (typically 30 days before)
  • The credential preview page shows the expiry date prominently
  • Verification links will indicate "expired" status after the date passes
  • Members can renew by completing update requirements or re-enrolling in training

This creates a recurring engagement loop—your members stay connected to your agency to maintain their credentials.

💡 Pro Tip

Use credential expiry strategically. A 2-year expiry period gives members time to prove value while creating a natural renewal cycle. You can charge a renewal fee or bundle it with advanced training—another revenue stream for your agency.

Why Modern Certificate Design Builds Agency Credibility

You might think certificate design is cosmetic. It's not. Here's why modern credentials matter for your bottom line.

First Impressions Compound

When a prospect sees your agency's credentials on a member's LinkedIn profile, their brain makes an instant judgment: "This agency's training must be legit." Sleek, modern design signals professionalism. Outdated PDFs signal the opposite.

Verification Builds Trust

GoHighLevel's verification feature is incredibly powerful for B2B agencies. When a prospect can click a link and instantly confirm someone is certified, objection rates drop dramatically. It's like the digital version of checking a license with the state board.

Shareable Credentials = Free Marketing

This is the kicker: every shared credential is a micro-endorsement of your agency. Your members become brand ambassadors without you asking. One certified member sharing their credential might reach 500 LinkedIn contacts—and that's organic reach money can't buy.

Competitive Differentiation

Most agencies still issue boring certificates. By investing in modern, verifiable credentials, you immediately stand out. Your members feel prouder of their achievement. Prospects see a more professional operation. It's a win across the board.

The agencies scaling to 6-7 figures aren't cutting corners on credibility signals. They understand that every touchpoint—including certificates—shapes how the market perceives them.
